pm_decision_extract.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short entry decision for SHFS: 16.97x relative volume spike post-split. Catalyst narrative structurally absent; no news, no SEC filings, no corroborating alt-data. Negative fundamentals (PE=-0.63, margin=-17.36%) support bear thesis. Macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation) disfavors low-quality shorts. Evidence is partial but preponderance favors watch posture pending catalyst confirmation.

actual_catalyst_narrative (no company claim present in packet)
pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: SHFS exhibits a real surge (16.97x relative volume, +2.29% 5d return) following a 1:20 stock split on 2025-03-24. However, the catalyst narrative is structurally absent: (1) news_event_summary returns 0 items <= cutoff (polygon_news wired, returned 0 rows — informative absence); (2) filing_confirmation not_evaluated (sec_8k_fulltext unavailable); (3) fundamental_snapshot status=insufficient_evidence (all fields null); (4) GitHub adapter failed (unmapped_ticker); (5) analyst sentiment flat (1 Buy, no upgrades); (6) Wikipedia pageviews show modest activity (peak 14 views on 2025-03-30, declining to 1 view by decision date). The structural absence of hard-to-fake evidence (news, SEC filings, operational metrics) relative to the price/volume action is the primary bear-thesis signal per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING and the asymmetric absence rule. The surge appears momentum-driven (post-split technical rebalancing) rather than value-driven. Negative fundamentals (PE=-0.63, net margin=-17.36%) support a bear thesis, but macro regime (Strengthening/Expansionary with strict_quality_and_valuation discipline) disfavors low-quality shorts. Upstream agents: Narrative Agent flags 'unsupported_claim' (vague narrative, no concrete catalyst); Alt-Data Verify flags 'narrative_contradicted' (no corroborating hard evidence); Surge-Short Agent recommends 'watch' (evidence partial but consistent with momentum pattern); Risk Agent confirms no veto conditions tripped. The preponderance of evidence favors a WATCH posture: the bear thesis is coherent (structural absence of catalyst, negative fundamentals, momentum-driven surge), but evidence is incomplete (no actual catalyst claim to definitively refute, fundamental data unavailable). Escalate to SHORT only if post-cutoff catalyst confirmation emerges and is validated by alt-data. Exit watch if catalyst is debunked or fundamentals improve.
invalidation_conditions: If an SEC 8-K or press release emerges post-cutoff confirming a material event (M&A, partnership, product launch, regulatory approval), the bear thesis is weakened and watch should be maintained pending alt-data corroboration.
If alt-data adapters (GitHub, clinical trials, regulatory filings) show corroborating activity post-cutoff, momentum thesis weakens and watch should be maintained.
If fundamental snapshot becomes available and shows positive revenue/earnings growth, valuation may be justified and watch should be maintained.
If analyst upgrades or institutional buying emerges post-cutoff, momentum may sustain and watch should be maintained.
If the stock split is revealed to be a forward split (not reverse), distress signal is weakened.

pm_decision_extract.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The packet does not include sector/industry classification for SHFS, making sector-aware reasoning difficult. The narrative_price_gap_assessment block is not_evaluated (pending agent LLM reasoning). Without the actual catalyst narrative, the PM cannot definitively assess whether the surge is momentum or value-driven. The packet structure assumes a news_event_summary or filing_confirmation will populate the catalyst; when both are absent/empty, the PM must reason from structural absence alone. This is defensible under §13.6.SOFT_REASONING and the universal-soft-veto principle, but a stronger signal would require the actual catalyst claim to be present in the packet (even if disputed).
recommended_followups: Obtain the actual catalyst narrative (press release, 8-K, or news article) that drove the surge on 2025-04-21.
Verify SHFS's business model and sector (currently unknown from packet); determine what alt-data adapters should be wired.
Check SEC EDGAR for 10-K/10-Q filings to assess whether the company is a legitimate operating entity or a shell/SPAC.
Confirm whether the 1:20 split was a reverse split; if so, assess post-split technical rebalancing vs. catalyst-driven surge.
Monitor for post-cutoff 8-K filings, press releases, or analyst upgrades that may validate or invalidate the bear thesis.
Assess institutional ownership and analyst coverage; flat sentiment suggests low institutional interest, which may support a short thesis.
If watch is maintained beyond 5 trading days without catalyst confirmation, escalate to PM for manual review or exit decision.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: STRUCTURAL ABSENCE PATTERN ANALYSIS: The evidence packet shows a severe price collapse (return_20d_pct=-57.52%, return_60d_pct=-70.81%) with a 1:20 stock split on 2025-03-24 and elevated relative volume (16.97x 20d average) on the decision date. However, the news_event_summary block is entirely empty (0 items in window; 20 items dropped post-cutoff due to publishedDate > allowed_data_cutoff). This is the critical structural absence: a stock experiencing a 70%+ decline over 60 days should have corroborating news coverage within the 7-day lookback window (polygon_news returned 0 rows). The absence of news is NOT silent—polygon_news adapter explicitly returned 0 items for [2025-04-14, 2025-04-21]. No SEC 8-K filing confirmation is available (filing_confirmation.status=not_evaluated, SEC_8K_confirmation=null). Fundamental data is entirely unavailable (fundamental_snapshot.status=insufficient_evidence, all snapshot_quarter fields null). Alternative data shows only Wikipedia pageview activity (peak 14 views on 2025-03-30, declining to 1 view by 2025-04-21)—this is not a catalyst signal but ambient attention noise. Analyst sentiment (fmp_sentiment) shows only 1 Buy rating consistently from 2023-2024, with no recent changes. The information_integrity_assessment explicitly flags 'low_tier_only' evidence (T3=1, T4=1; T1=0, T2=0) and marks use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false due to pollution-defense layer not implemented. SECTOR REASONING: SHFS appears to be a micro-cap equity (price $2.23, market cap likely <$100M based on volume). For a company of this size and sector classification (unknown from packet), the absence of news coverage during a 70% drawdown is itself a signal—either (1) the move is driven by technical/liquidity factors unrelated to fundamental catalysts, (2) the company is in a distressed state with minimal disclosure, or (3) the price action is a data artifact (e.g., reverse split adjustment). The 1:20 split on 2025-03-24 is a corporate action, not a catalyst for value creation. NARRATIVE ASSESSMENT: No concrete company claim is present in the packet. No specific dollar amounts, customer names, product launches, regulatory filings, or partnership announcements are documented. The only observable facts are: (1) stock split, (2) severe price decline, (3) elevated volume, (4) minimal analyst coverage (1 Buy, no recent changes), (5) no news corroboration. This is a VAGUE / UNSUPPORTED_CLAIM scenario: the price action exists, but the underlying catalyst is unidentified and unverified. The structural absence of news coverage relative to the magnitude of the price move is a bear-thesis-leaning signal per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING—when a stock falls 70% in 60 days and there is zero news coverage in the 7-day window, either the move is not catalyst-driven or the catalyst is not being disclosed.
invalidation_conditions: If news_event_summary items become available post-cutoff and reveal a specific, concrete catalyst (e.g., FDA rejection, customer loss, financing event), the vague_narrative_flag should be downgraded and catalyst_specificity_score should be re-evaluated.
If SEC 8-K filing is discovered with a material event disclosure (e.g., bankruptcy, going-concern warning, asset sale), the verdict should shift to 'supported' or 'credible_catalyst'.
If fundamental_snapshot data becomes available and shows a specific trigger (e.g., massive revenue miss, covenant breach), the assessment should be revised.
If the stock split is revealed to be a reverse split (1:20 reverse, not forward), this is a distress signal and should be explicitly flagged as such.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure is sound, but the v1 generator has several unimplemented layers: (1) information_integrity_assessment.pollution_defense_layer is not implemented; fail-closed to use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false. (2) filing_confirmation is not_evaluated because sec_edgar and sec_8k_fulltext adapters are not wired. (3) fundamental_snapshot is insufficient_evidence because income/balance/cashflow adapters returned no PIT-safe rows. (4) news_event_summary has 0 items in window but 20 items dropped post-cutoff; the agent cannot access those 20 items to determine if a catalyst exists. These are architectural gaps, not agent weaknesses. The agent's role is to classify the catalyst from available evidence; when evidence is structurally absent, the agent must reason about the absence pattern itself (per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING). In this case, the absence of news coverage during a 70% decline is itself a signal—either the move is technical/liquidity-driven, or the catalyst is not being disclosed.

alt_data_verify.decision_or_assessment: Narrative-vs-Evidence Verification for SHFS surge candidate
alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: SHFS exhibits a 16.97x relative volume spike on 2025-04-17 (2.16M shares vs 127k avg 20d) with price rising from $1.92 to $2.23 (+16.1% intraday). A 1:20 stock split occurred 2025-03-24, preceding the surge by ~24 days. Wikipedia pageviews show modest attention spike (peak 14 views on 2025-03-30, declining to 1-6 views by decision date), consistent with retail/meme-stock pattern. However, NO corroborating hard-to-fake evidence is present: (1) news_event_summary returns 0 items <= cutoff (polygon_news wired, returned 0 rows — informative absence); (2) filing_confirmation not_connected (sec_8k_fulltext unavailable); (3) fundamental_snapshot status=insufficient_evidence (no PIT-safe quarterly data); (4) github_public failed with unmapped_ticker (not sector-relevant for non-tech, but signals weak alt-data footprint); (5) fmp_sentiment shows only analyst grades (T2, descriptor-only), no news sentiment; (6) information_integrity_assessment flags source_tier_distribution T1=0 T2=0 T3=1 T4=1, with use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false due to pollution-defense layer unimplemented. The 20d return of -57.5% and 60d return of -70.8% indicate the surge is a rebound from severe prior decline, not a new catalyst-driven move. Macro regime is Strengthening/Expansionary (stress_score=1), which is neutral-to-supportive for equities but does not explain SHFS-specific surge. The absence of news, SEC filings, and operational corroboration relative to the price/volume action is the primary bear-thesis signal: structural absence of hard evidence contradicts the narrative that this is a fundamental or catalyst-driven move. The Wikipedia attention is low-tier (T4/T5 equivalent) and does not constitute primary corroboration. Analyst grades are stale (last update 2024-11-01) and show only 1 Buy rating, no recent upgrade.
invalidation_conditions: If a positive SEC 8-K filing or press release emerges post-decision with material catalyst (partnership, funding, product launch, regulatory approval)
If volume sustains above 500k shares/day with price holding above $2.50 for 3+ consecutive days (suggests institutional accumulation, not retail meme)
If analyst upgrades or earnings beat materialize within 5 trading days
If GitHub or other tech-activity data becomes available and shows active development (if SHFS is a software/tech company)

alt_data_verify.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The packet does not include sector/industry classification for SHFS, making sector-aware reasoning difficult. The alternative_data_features block is a placeholder with no industry-specific adapters wired (all marked not_connected). The information_integrity_assessment is also a placeholder with pollution-defense layer unimplemented, forcing use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false even when T2 data is present. These gaps limit the depth of alt-data verification.
recommended_followups: Obtain SHFS sector/industry classification (e.g., from SEC 10-K Item 1 or FactSet) to calibrate expected alt-data footprint
Wire industry-specific adapters (e.g., if SHFS is biotech, wire clinical-trial registry; if software, wire GitHub; if retail, wire store-count)
Implement pollution-defense layer in information_integrity_assessment to allow primary-signal use when T1/T2 corroboration is present
Fetch sec_8k_fulltext and sec_form4 to detect recent catalysts and insider trading activity
Extend news_event_summary lookback or use alternative news sources to capture pre-cutoff catalysts
Obtain fundamental_snapshot with PIT-safe quarterly data to assess operational health and profitability
Monitor post-decision price/volume action: if volume sustains and price holds, escalate to PM for manual review
alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: SHFS exhibits a 16.97x volume spike and 16% intraday price move on 2025-04-17, but NO corroborating hard-to-fake evidence (news, filings, operational metrics, tech activity) is present. The structural absence of news (0 items <= cutoff), SEC filings (not_connected), and fundamental data (insufficient_evidence) relative to the price/volume action is the primary contradiction signal. Wikipedia attention is low-tier (peak 14 views, declining to 1-6 by decision date) and does not constitute primary corroboration. The surge appears to be a rebound from a -70.8% 60d decline, not a new catalyst-driven move. Macro regime is neutral (Strengthening/Expansionary, stress_score=1). The narrative-price gap is HIGH because the market is pricing in a significant move without visible hard evidence to support it.

Filing_confirmation not_connected (sec_8k_fulltext unavailable). Fundamental_snapshot status=insufficient_evidence (no PIT-safe quarterly data). GitHub_public failed with unmapped_ticker. FMP_sentiment shows only stale analyst grades (last update 2024-11-01, 5+ months before decision). No insider trading (Form 4), institutional ownership (13F), or regulatory filings detected. The structural absence of hard evidence relative to the price/volume action is itself a strong contradiction signal for a surge-short candidate.

surge_short.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short evaluation for SHFS: 16.97x relative volume spike, +2.29% 5d return post-split. Catalyst narrative absent; no news, no SEC filings, no corroborating alt-data. Structural absence of expected evidence for a listed-equity surge is bear-thesis signal.
evidence_used: sentiment_community_ownership_evidence.fmp_sentiment (22 rows, 1 Buy rating, no Strong Buy/Sell)
evidence_missing: fundamental_snapshot.revenue, net_income, operating_income, ebitda, eps (all null)
surge_short.reasoning_summary: SHFS surged on 2025-04-21 with 16.97x relative volume and +2.29% 5d return following a 1:20 stock split on 2025-03-24. The surge is real (price and volume data are solid). However, the catalyst narrative is structurally absent. For a listed US equity claiming a material event, the expected evidence footprint should include: (1) SEC 8-K or press release, (2) news coverage, (3) corroborating alt-data (GitHub activity for tech, clinical trials for biotech, etc.). SHFS exhibits NONE of these. News adapter (polygon_news) is wired and returned 0 rows. Filing adapter (sec_8k) is not connected but no 8-K rows are present in filing_confirmation. GitHub adapter failed (unmapped_ticker). Wikipedia pageviews show modest activity (peak 14 views on 2025-03-30, trailing off to 1 view on 2025-04-21). Analyst sentiment is flat (1 Buy, no Strong Buy/Sell across 22 historical records). Fundamentals are unavailable (all snapshot fields null), but TTM ratios show negative earnings (PE=-0.63, net margin=-17.36%). The macro regime is Strengthening/Expansionary with strict_quality_and_valuation discipline, which argues against low-quality or momentum-driven shorts. The structural absence of corroborating evidence relative to the public narrative (a surge on a catalyst) is the bear-thesis signal. The surge appears to be momentum/technical (post-split volume spike) rather than value creation. However, evidence is PARTIAL: we lack the actual catalyst narrative (news_event_summary is empty, filing_confirmation not evaluated), so we cannot definitively rule out a real event that simply failed to propagate to our adapters. Confidence is MEDIUM because the absence pattern is consistent but not conclusive.
invalidation_conditions: If an SEC 8-K or press release emerges post-cutoff confirming a material event (M&A, partnership, product launch, regulatory approval), the bear thesis is invalidated.
If alt-data adapters (GitHub, clinical trials, regulatory filings) show corroborating activity post-cutoff, momentum thesis weakens.
If fundamental snapshot becomes available and shows positive revenue/earnings growth, valuation may be justified.
If analyst upgrades or institutional buying emerges post-cutoff, momentum may sustain.
risk_flags: Negative earnings (PE=-0.63, net margin=-17.36%) — company is unprofitable; short thesis is supported by fundamentals.
Macro regime is Strengthening/Expansionary with strict_quality_and_valuation discipline — low-quality shorts are disfavored.
Post-split volatility — 1:20 split on 2025-03-24 may have triggered technical rebalancing; surge may be mechanical, not catalyst-driven.
Structural absence of news/filings/alt-data is consistent with a micro-cap or shell company; liquidity risk on short exit.
Wikipedia pageviews peaked 2025-03-30 (14 views) and declined thereafter; attention is fading, not building.
Analyst sentiment is flat (1 Buy, no upgrades); no institutional support visible.
suggested_improvements: Obtain the actual catalyst narrative from the candidate's investor relations or press release archive (not in packet).
Verify whether SHFS is a shell company, SPAC, or legitimate operating company via SEC EDGAR 10-K/10-Q.
Check if the 1:20 split was a reverse split (common in distressed/low-price stocks); if so, the surge may be post-split technical rebalancing, not catalyst-driven.
Confirm whether GitHub mapping is available for SHFS; if it's a software/tech company, GitHub absence is a red flag.
Monitor for post-cutoff 8-K filings or press releases that may explain the surge.
Assess whether the company has any institutional ownership or analyst coverage; flat sentiment suggests low institutional interest.

surge_short.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The narrative_price_gap_assessment block is not_evaluated (pending agent LLM reasoning). Without the actual catalyst narrative, the surge-short agent cannot definitively assess whether the surge is momentum or value-driven. The packet structure assumes a news_event_summary or filing_confirmation will populate the catalyst; when both are absent/empty, the agent must reason from structural absence alone. This is defensible under §13.6.SOFT_REASONING and the universal-soft-veto principle, but a stronger signal would require the actual catalyst claim to be present in the packet (even if disputed).
recommended_followups: Obtain the actual catalyst narrative (press release, 8-K, or news article) that drove the surge on 2025-04-21.
Verify SHFS's business model and sector (currently unknown from packet); determine what alt-data adapters should be wired.
Check SEC EDGAR for 10-K/10-Q filings to assess whether the company is a legitimate operating entity or a shell/SPAC.
Confirm whether the 1:20 split was a reverse split; if so, assess post-split technical rebalancing vs. catalyst-driven surge.
Monitor for post-cutoff 8-K filings, press releases, or analyst upgrades that may validate or invalidate the bear thesis.
Assess institutional ownership and analyst coverage; flat sentiment suggests low institutional interest, which may support a short thesis.

risk.decision_or_assessment: Hard-veto evaluation for surge_short candidate SHFS per §5.13
risk.reasoning_summary: Mechanical evaluation of §5.13 hard-veto conditions (a)-(e) for surge_short candidate SHFS. Condition (a) equity-cap breach: not applicable to surge_short per §5.13(a) scope (applies to quality_long only). Condition (b) short-sleeve cap breach: candidate_type=surge_short; no upstream sleeve position data provided in packet; cannot evaluate breach without current sleeve state; however, packet does not indicate breach condition. Condition (c) critical data_quality_flags: examined all data_quality_flags blocks; no flag has severity='critical'. Condition (d) anti-pollution integrity veto: candidate_type=surge_short; per §5.17 RELAXED 2026-05-06, §5.13(d) is INAPPLICABLE to surge_short candidates. Condition (e) lookahead veto: envelope.data_after_cutoff_used=false AND envelope.lookahead_safe=true; lookahead veto does NOT trip. Result: NO VETO CONDITIONS TRIPPED. Candidate passes hard-veto gate.
invalidation_conditions: If upstream sleeve position data becomes available and shows short sleeve already at or above 10% cap, condition (b) would trip.
If any data_quality_flag is retroactively marked severity='critical', condition (c) would trip.
If envelope.data_after_cutoff_used is changed to true or envelope.lookahead_safe is changed to false, condition (e) would trip.

suggested_improvements: Provide upstream sleeve position data (current short exposure pct) to enable full evaluation of condition (b) short-sleeve cap breach.

pm.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short entry decision for SHFS: 16.97x relative volume spike post-split. Catalyst narrative structurally absent; no news, no SEC filings, no corroborating alt-data. Negative fundamentals (PE=-0.63, margin=-17.36%) support bear thesis. Macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation) disfavors low-quality shorts. Evidence is partial but preponderance favors watch posture pending catalyst confirmation.

pm.reasoning_summary: SHFS exhibits a real surge (16.97x relative volume, +2.29% 5d return) following a 1:20 stock split on 2025-03-24. However, the catalyst narrative is structurally absent: (1) news_event_summary returns 0 items <= cutoff (polygon_news wired, returned 0 rows — informative absence); (2) filing_confirmation not_evaluated (sec_8k_fulltext unavailable); (3) fundamental_snapshot status=insufficient_evidence (all fields null); (4) GitHub adapter failed (unmapped_ticker); (5) analyst sentiment flat (1 Buy, no upgrades); (6) Wikipedia pageviews show modest activity (peak 14 views on 2025-03-30, declining to 1 view by decision date). The structural absence of hard-to-fake evidence (news, SEC filings, operational metrics) relative to the price/volume action is the primary bear-thesis signal per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING and the asymmetric absence rule. The surge appears momentum-driven (post-split technical rebalancing) rather than value-driven. Negative fundamentals (PE=-0.63, net margin=-17.36%) support a bear thesis, but macro regime (Strengthening/Expansionary with strict_quality_and_valuation discipline) disfavors low-quality shorts. Upstream agents: Narrative Agent flags 'unsupported_claim' (vague narrative, no concrete catalyst); Alt-Data Verify flags 'narrative_contradicted' (no corroborating hard evidence); Surge-Short Agent recommends 'watch' (evidence partial but consistent with momentum pattern); Risk Agent confirms no veto conditions tripped. The preponderance of evidence favors a WATCH posture: the bear thesis is coherent (structural absence of catalyst, negative fundamentals, momentum-driven surge), but evidence is incomplete (no actual catalyst claim to definitively refute, fundamental data unavailable). Escalate to SHORT only if post-cutoff catalyst confirmation emerges and is validated by alt-data. Exit watch if catalyst is debunked or fundamentals improve.
invalidation_conditions: If an SEC 8-K or press release emerges post-cutoff confirming a material event (M&A, partnership, product launch, regulatory approval), the bear thesis is weakened and watch should be maintained pending alt-data corroboration.
If alt-data adapters (GitHub, clinical trials, regulatory filings) show corroborating activity post-cutoff, momentum thesis weakens and watch should be maintained.
If fundamental snapshot becomes available and shows positive revenue/earnings growth, valuation may be justified and watch should be maintained.
If analyst upgrades or institutional buying emerges post-cutoff, momentum may sustain and watch should be maintained.
If the stock split is revealed to be a forward split (not reverse), distress signal is weakened.
